description_long: This house features a prominent dark gable roof with intersecting sections, creating a cross-gabled appearance. The exterior walls are a light gray, contrasting with the deep, nearly black roof. The front facade includes a central protruding section with two upper windows and two lower windows, suggesting a symmetrical design. The side and rear elevations display additional window placements, with the rear having a smaller gable and fewer windows. The structure appears to be a two-story residential building, likely constructed from concrete or masonry with a shingled or metal roof. The overall design is simple and functional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on geometric forms.
description: A two-story house with a dark cross-gabled roof and light gray walls, featuring symmetrical window arrangements and a central front projection.
description_view_0: Front view shows the central gabled projection with two upper and two lower windows, highlighting the main entrance facade.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the cross-gabled roof and the symmetrical window arrangement on the side elevation.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a smaller gable with two small windows and additional side windows, less prominent than the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the other side, emphasizing the cross-gabled roof and symmetrical window placement.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with a gabled overhang, no visible porch or columns, and a simple, functional design.
